msa.title: Uptime SLA tightened to 99.9% with 10-business-day notice
msa.detail: Initech raised the uptime requirement from 99.5% to 99.9% and stretched maintenance notice from 48 hours to ten business days, with a hard exclusion on business-hours maintenance. Operationally heavy for a startup ops team.

msa.title: Support upgraded to 24/7/365 with 1h critical response
msa.detail: Replaces business-hours support with 24/7 coverage, tiered response/resolution SLAs (1h response, 4h resolution for critical). Requires a pager rotation Dunder does not currently staff.

msa.title: Liability cap carve-out renders breach exposure uncapped
msa.detail: Cap of 12 months' fees is preserved, but data-breach and §9 claims are explicitly excluded from the cap, leaving Vendor with effectively uncapped exposure on the highest-frequency claim type for a SaaS deal. Existential risk for a startup balance sheet.

msa.title: Consequential damages carve-outs for breach + gross negligence
msa.detail: Mutual exclusion of consequentials retained, but with carve-outs for data breach and gross negligence. Layered with R03 this compounds breach exposure.

msa.title: Client claims ownership of all custom-built deliverables
msa.detail: Client takes ownership of any feature built or funded by Client, including improvements that would otherwise generalize across the Vendor's roadmap. Erodes Vendor's product moat.

msa.title: Feedback usage gated by Client consent
msa.detail: Vendor cannot use Client feedback to build features for other clients without consent. Manageable but slows the iteration loop.

msa.title: Net 60 + full-invoice withholding during dispute
msa.detail: Stretches payment to Net 60, drops late-payment interest to 0.5%/mo, and allows Client to withhold the entire invoice (not just disputed portion) during a good-faith dispute, while requiring Vendor to keep services running.

msa.title: Fee increase capped at 3% with 90-day notice
msa.detail: Drops the renewal fee-increase ceiling from 5% to 3% and stretches notice from 60 to 90 days. Modest commercial drag.

msa.title: Convenience termination + 15-day cure for material breach
msa.detail: Adds a Client-only convenience termination on 30 days' notice and shortens the cure period for material breach from 30 to 15 days. Forecasting risk for the Vendor.

msa.title: Bi-annual audit rights with 48 hours notice
msa.detail: Adds 2x/year on-site audit rights with 48-hour notice. Standard for regulated FIs, operationally heavy for a small Vendor.

msa.title: Ontario-only data residency
msa.detail: Restricts data residency from 'Canada' to 'Ontario only', with an outright bar on cross-border without consent. Constrains DR/HA topology and any analytics subprocessor in another province or jurisdiction.

msa.title: Insurance minimums: $5M CGL, $5M E&O, $10M cyber
msa.detail: Sets insurance minimums well above what most Series-A vendors carry. Cyber liability at $10M is the binding constraint.

msa.title: Client step-in rights on Vendor performance failure
msa.detail: Grants Client the right to assume control of Services on Vendor performance failure. Effectively a takeover trigger; existential governance issue for Vendor.

nda.title: Converted to one-way NDA (Vendor as recipient only)
nda.detail: Initech replaced the mutual NDA with a one-way structure under which only the Vendor accepts confidentiality obligations.

nda.title: Confidential information scope expanded to all disclosures
nda.detail: Removed the 'reasonable person' standard. Confidential information now includes everything disclosed, marked or unmarked, oral or written.

nda.title: Injunctive relief without bond for Initech
nda.detail: Removes the bond requirement for Initech only when seeking injunctive relief. Asymmetric remedy.

nda.title: Term + survival stretched to 3 + 5 years
nda.detail: Stretches the NDA term from 2 years to 3 years and survival from 2 to 5 years, with asymmetric termination.

nda.title: No archival copy without consent + written certification
nda.detail: Eliminates the standard backup/archival carve-out and requires written certification of destruction.
